title: We already set container=podman environment variable
body:
Only need to set container, no need for containers

Signed-off-by: Daniel J Walsh <dwalsh@redhat.com>

comments.body: Does it hurt to leave it other than the cost of the check?  Seems like a nice fail-safe if we ever mess up the envvar definition.
I think the check needs to remain, but should be `"container"` instead of `"containers"` for consistency.

@containers/podman-maintainers PTAL
This is ready to go in.

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *rhatdan*, *saschagrunert* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

LGTM but is the `""` check really the desired intention? That is: do you want to allow a user to override it?
```console
$ ./bin/podman run -it --rm -e container=foo $iii sh
container# echo $container
foo    <--- should podman allow user to override?
```
@edsantiago Yes, we want to allow users to override the environment variable, we should only be setting it iff the user and image did NOT set it.
